Data Sourcing and Generation Steps:

1. GENERATE IMAGES VIA API
   Source: https://facestudio.app/api-access
   Script: external/facestudio/accumulate_faces.py
   Command: python3 external/facestudio/accumulate_faces.py
   Output: external/facestudio/faces/<gender>_age<NN>_<ethnicity>[_<NN>].jpeg

   The script iterates the cross-product of:
     Gender:    male, female
     Age:       20, 25, 30, 35, 40, 45, 50, 55, 60, 65, 70  (5-year steps, 20–70 inclusive)
     Ethnicity: european, african, west_asian, south_asian,
                east_asian, southeast_asian, latin_american
   Count:       N images per combination (default 3), indexed _01, _02, _03

   Total combinations: 11 ages × 2 genders × 7 ethnicities × 3 = 462 images

2. RATE LIMITS
   FaceStudio enforces approximately 60 requests per day on free-tier accounts.
   The script handles HTTP 429 (Too Many Requests) by printing a clear message
   and exiting cleanly. Re-run the script the next day; it skips already-
   downloaded files and continues from where it left off.

3. ACCUMULATION APPROACH
   Run the script daily until all combinations are downloaded.
   At 60 requests/day: ~8 days for 462 images.
   The script randomises the download order so early runs yield good variety
   across gender, age, and ethnicity rather than exhausting one slice first.

4. SAVE TO REPOSITORY
   Target directory: external/facestudio/faces/
   File naming: <gender>_age<NN>_<ethnicity>_<NN>.jpeg
     e.g. female_age25_african_01.jpeg
          male_age40_european_02.jpeg
   Commit: git add external/facestudio/faces/
           git commit -m "[data] Add FaceStudio face images (batch N)"

5. GENERATE SQL POPULATE SCRIPT  (future)
   Script: projects/ores.codegen/src/images_generate_sql.py
   Command: python3 images_generate_sql.py --config facestudio
   Output: projects/ores.sql/populate/faces/

LICENCE
-------
Source:  https://facestudio.app/terms  (reviewed 2026-06-10)

Key clauses:
  Clause 5: Generated images may be used by Face Studio for R&D. No explicit
            restriction on storing images in a repository or committing to VCS.
  Clause 7: Each user may have one active account; circumventing user limits
            is prohibited (run the script under one account only).
  Clause 8: You may not use Face Studio output to create a duplicate product
            or offer a similar face-generation service. This restriction does
            NOT apply to using faces as profile-picture assets in an
            unrelated application (ORE Studio is a trading-system, not a
            face-generation service).

Summary: Permitted for internal use as profile pictures in the ORE Studio
seeder. No attribution required. Do not redistribute as a face-generation
dataset or build a competing face-generation service.

NAMING SCHEME
-------------
  <gender>_age<NN>_<ethnicity>_<index>.jpeg
    gender:    male | female
    age NN:    two-digit age (20–70)
    ethnicity: european | african | west_asian | south_asian |
               east_asian | southeast_asian | latin_american
    index:     two-digit sequence number (01, 02, 03, …)

  Examples:
    female_age25_african_01.jpeg
    male_age40_south_asian_02.jpeg
    female_age70_european_01.jpeg

NOTE ON INITIAL BATCH
---------------------
The first 60 images (downloaded before the script fixes) use the older
single-image naming convention (no index suffix, ages 0–80 in 5-year steps).
These are present in external/facestudio/faces/ for historical completeness
but will be superseded as the corrected script accumulates indexed images in
the 20–70 age range.
